title: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints
exclusions: Does NOT validate key rotation (covered by 22.41.3); focuses on transport posture at backup ingress.
evidence: Saved search 'backup_tls_posture' (daily) reports any backup repository endpoint with legacy protocol or near-expiry certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.12 backup policies include protection of transport.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.12 (Backup policies and recovery methods) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.4: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.4' running on TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA transmission security applies to backup ingress.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA §164.312(e)(1) (Transmission security) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.4: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.4' running on TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 CP-9(3) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.4: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.4' running on TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Backup repo at backup.example.com negotiates TLS 1.0; the UC fires daily.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All backup endpoints negotiate TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

dataSources: TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory.
spl:
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-24h
| search host IN (`backup_repository_endpoints`)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
description: Ensures the transport to backup repositories — traditionally neglected — matches the same TLS posture as customer-facing systems.
value: Closes a gap pattern observed in DORA inspections where the backup plane had older TLS than the production plane.
implementation: (1) Inventory backup endpoints; (2) scan daily; (3) on finding, ticket to platform engineering. Indexes required: `index=tls`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tls:scan`.
visualization: Table of endpoints with weak posture, time chart of scan results, single-value '% of backup endpoints with strong TLS'. Key fields: `cipher`, `days_to_expiry`, `host`, `protocol`.
knownFalsePositives: Backup test failures during quarterly DR drills, vendor-driven restoration tests, or planned platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Inventory backup endpoints; (2) scan daily; (3) on finding, ticket to platform engineering.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-24h
| search host IN (`backup_repository_endpoints`)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
```

Understanding this SPL

**Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints** — Closes a gap pattern observed in DORA inspections where the backup plane had older TLS than the production plane.

Documented **Data sources**: TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: tls; **sourcetype**: tls:scan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=tls, sourcetype=tls:scan, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_expiry** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: cipher, days_to_expiry, host, protocol.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of endpoints with weak posture, time chart of scan results, single-value '% of backup endpoints with strong TLS'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show backups were tested and restorable when regulators or disaster recovery need proof.
